statement.markdown: In a Trie data structure, you are given a string and asked to delete it. However, the current implementation has a logical bug that causes incorrect deletion. Your task is to identify and fix this bug.
statement.inputFormat: The input will be a string of lowercase English letters and a string array of lowercase English letters representing the words to be inserted or deleted.
statement.outputFormat: Your function should return true if the deletion is successful, false otherwise.
statement.notes: The trie is initialized with some words, and you should not modify these words.

examples.explanation: The word 'apple' should not be deleted.

hints.text: Think about how deletion would affect the children of a node.

editorial.title: Editorial: Incorrect Trie Deletion
editorial.intuition: When deleting a word from the Trie, we should check if the current node is the end of a word. If it is, we should not delete it until its children are also removed.
editorial.bruteForce: We can try deleting each word individually and check if the result is correct.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution involves correctly handling the deletion of the children nodes of the end node.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The correctness of the solution can be proven by checking all possible cases.
editorial.dryRun: Step-by-step example run can be found in the provided solutions.
commonMistakes: Mistake 1: Incorrect handling of end nodes

functionSignature.java: public boolean deleteWord(TrieNode root, String word);
functionSignature.python: def deleteWord(self, root: TrieNode, word: str) -> bool:
